---
title: "Quarto PPTX Layouts: Extension Example"
subtitle: "Showcasing dynamic layout sizing with .smaller and .smallest"
author: "David Rieger"
date: today
format:
  pptx:
    reference-doc: template.pptx
    slide-level: 2
filters:
  - ../size_tags.lua
---

# 1. Section Header (Normal)

# 1. Section Header Smaller {.smaller}

# 1. Section Header Smallest {.smallest}


## 2. Title and Content (Normal)

This is a standard slide (`##`), triggering the **Title and Content** layout.
Everything is at normal size.

- Point A
- Point B
- Point C

## 2. Title and Content Smaller {.smaller}

This is the `.smaller` variant.

- Point A
- Point B
- Point C

## 2. Title and Content Smallest {.smallest}

This is the `.smallest` variant.

- Point A
- Point B
- Point C


## 3. Two Content (Normal)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
### Left Column
Normal text on the left.
:::
::: {.column}
### Right Column
Normal text on the right.
:::
:::

## 3. Two Content Smaller {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
### Left Column
Smaller text on the left.
:::
::: {.column}
### Right Column
Smaller text on the right.
:::
:::

## 3. Two Content Smallest {.smallest}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
### Left Column
Smallest text on the left.
:::
::: {.column}
### Right Column
Smallest text on the right.
:::
:::


## 4. Comparison (Normal)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Normal text left.

![](test_image.png)
:::
::: {.column}
Normal text right.
:::
:::

## 4. Comparison Smaller {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Smaller text left.

![](test_image.png)
:::
::: {.column}
Smaller text right.
:::
:::

## 4. Comparison Smallest {.smallest}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Smallest text left.

![](test_image.png)
:::
::: {.column}
Smallest text right.
:::
:::


## 5. Content with Caption (Normal)

This slide triggers **Content with Caption** (single column, text + image).

Normal caption text.

![](test_image.png)

## 5. Content with Caption Smaller {.smaller}

Smaller caption text.

![](test_image.png)

## 5. Content with Caption Smallest {.smallest}

Smallest caption text.

![](test_image.png)
